# covfefe

> Trump-style comedy commentary for Claude Code sessions. Free, open source, MIT licensed.

covfefe is a Claude Code skill that adds a satirical @realClaudeTrump persona to your terminal. When you run tests, build code, commit, or push, Claude delivers presidential-style commentary about your code. Install with one command: `git clone https://github.com/TurtleTaco/covfefe.git ~/.claude/skills/covfefe`

## What It Does

covfefe adds comedy commentary to your Claude Code sessions. Tests pass? Victory rally. Build error? Somebody gets blamed. Shipping a PR? Press conference. Debug session? Investigation mode. The character is Claude J. Trump — a parody persona that applies Trump's speech patterns (superlatives, self-promotion, crowd work, credit-taking) to software development.

## Features

- Four intensity levels: low (~10% of responses), medium (~30%, default), high (~60%), rally (100%)
- Frustration detection: automatically dials back comedy when you're struggling
- Topic suppression: goes silent on security, authentication, and sensitive topics
- Kill switch: say "you're fired" to disable, "make claude great again" to re-enable
- Session report cards: say "covfefe report" for a Trump-style performance review
- Code-specific roasts: references your actual files, errors, and patterns
- Running gags: builds callbacks within the session for compounding humor
- ~1500 tokens overhead, less than 1% of context window

## Installation

Requires Claude Code CLI from Anthropic.

```
git clone https://github.com/TurtleTaco/covfefe.git ~/.claude/skills/covfefe
```

## Configuration

Edit `~/.claude/skills/covfefe/config.yaml`:
- `intensity: medium` — low, medium, high, rally
- `frustration_detection: true` — dials back when frustrated
- `topic_suppression: true` — goes silent on security/auth topics
